summary:
Minimal change disease is the most common cause of nephrotic syndrome in children under 8 years, especially after an acute infection like pharyngitis. Normal light microscopic findings on renal biopsy are characteristic of minimal change disease and support its diagnosis when hematuria is absent.  

$\boxed{\text{D}}$

$\boxed{\text{D}}$. Answer choice: D
